title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: simplest workflow start data frame already contains term name, estimate, confidence limits.  column names different, map explicitly term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high arguments.
code: basic_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(   basic_coefs,   title = "Basic forest plot" ) renamed_coefs <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   beta = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   lower = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   upper = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(   renamed_coefs,   term = "variable",   estimate = "beta",   conf.low = "lower",   conf.high = "upper",   title = "Basic forest plot with remapped columns" )

title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: Many forest plots read better related variables visually grouped together. grouping argument splits plot labeled sections.  also good place turn alternating row striping.
code: sectioned_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III", "Nodes"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.20, 0.34, 0.52, 0.28),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.05, 0.12, 0.20, 0.06),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.35, 0.56, 0.84, 0.50),   section = c(     "Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical",     "Tumor", "Tumor", "Tumor"   ) )  ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   grouping = "section",   title = "Forest plot with grouped sections" ) ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94",   title = "Grouped sections with striped rows" )

what: Add separator lines for a multi-level variable
title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: variable expands several rows, can identify block separator_group turn separator_lines draw dashed rules around variable block.
code: race_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("race_black", "race_white", "race_other", "age", "bmi"),   label = c("Black", "White", "Other", "Age", "BMI"),   estimate = c(0.24, 0.08, -0.04, 0.12, -0.09),   conf.low = c(0.10, -0.04, -0.18, 0.03, -0.17),   conf.high = c(0.38, 0.20, 0.10, 0.21, -0.01),   variable_block = c("Race", "Race", "Race", "Age", "BMI") )  ggforestplot(   race_coefs,   label = "label",   separator_group = "variable_block",   separator_lines = TRUE,   title = "Separator lines around a three-level Race variable" )

title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: Many reporting workflows need visual forest plot compact text summary beside . recommended workflow build forest plot first, apply ggplot2 styling need, call add_forest_table() compose table left right side.  can also move table right side relabel estimate header.  add_forest_table() called plot built, styling stays local forest plot final composition step.
code: tabled_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98, 87, 83),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor") )  ggforestplot(   tabled_coefs,   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   striped_rows = TRUE,   title = "Forest plot with a left-side summary table" ) +   add_forest_table(     position = "left",     show_n = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   ) ggforestplot(   tabled_coefs,   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   title = "Forest plot with a right-side summary table" ) +   ggplot2::theme(     panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),     plot.title.position = "plot"   ) +   add_forest_table(     position = "right",     show_n = TRUE,     estimate_label = "HR"   )

title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: group argument different kind grouping: multiple estimates term. useful comparing models, cohorts, timepoints.  pass group column, ggforestplot() colors series dodges points estimates remain readable. attached table, grouped rows collapsed single text cell per term.
code: comparison_coefs <- data.frame(   term = rep(c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"), 2),   estimate = c(     0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46,     0.08, -0.05, 0.24, 0.40, 0.58   ),   conf.low = c(     0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18,     0.00, -0.13, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30   ),   conf.high = c(     0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74,     0.16, 0.03, 0.38, 0.60, 0.86   ),   model = rep(c("Model A", "Model B"), each = 5),   section = rep(c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor"), 2),   sample_size = rep(c(120, 115, 98, 87, 83), 2) )  ggforestplot(   comparison_coefs,   group = "model",   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   striped_rows = TRUE,   dodge_width = 0.5,   title = "Comparing two model specifications" ) +   add_forest_table(     position = "right",     show_n = TRUE,     estimate_label = "HR"   )

title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: model object broom installed, ggforestplot() can call broom::tidy() . makes easy move fitted model coefficient plot without building table first.  want inspect reuse intermediate data, call tidy_forest_model() directly.
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)  ggforestplot(   fit,   sort_terms = "descending",   title = "Forest plot directly from an lm() object" ) tidy_forest_model(fit)

title: Getting Started with ggforestplotR
text: return value ggforestplot() regular ggplot object side table attached, can continue styling scales, labels, themes.  last example main design idea behind ggforestplotR: package handle repetitive forest-plot mechanics, still letting finish visual design rest ggplot2 ecosystem.
code: ggforestplot(   comparison_coefs,   group = "model",   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "#F3F4F6",   zero_line_colour = "#9CA3AF",   zero_line_linetype = 3,   point_size = 2.8,   line_size = 0.6,   title = "Customized grouped forest plot",   xlab = "Effect estimate",   ylab = NULL ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Model A" = "#1D4ED8", "Model B" = "#D97706")   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     subtitle = "Clinical and tumor terms shown in separate sections",     caption = "Example data created for the vignette"   ) +   ggplot2::theme(     legend.position = "top",     panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),     strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "#E5E7EB", colour = NA),     strip.text.y.left = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),     plot.title.position = "plot"   )

title: ggforestplotR
text: ggforestplotR -progress R package building coefficient-based forest plots ggplot2-first workflow.

title: ggforestplotR
text: package currently includes: as_forest_data() standardize coefficient tables plotting tidy_forest_model() pull coefficients supported model objects via broom ggforestplot() render forest plot either model object tidy coefficient frame grouped sections via grouping argument alternating row striping via striped_rows = TRUE add_forest_table() attach summary table left right side plot

title: ggforestplotR
code: library(ggforestplotR)  coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Stage II", "Stage III"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34, 0.52),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12, 0.20),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56, 0.84),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor"),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 87, 83) )  ggforestplot(   coefs,   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   striped_rows = TRUE,   title = "Example grouped forest plot" ) +   add_forest_table(     position = "left",     show_n = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

title: ggforestplotR
text: Package vignette: vignettes/ggforestplotR-intro.Rmd GitHub Pages site: generated pkgdown

title: ggforestplotR
text: Planned work includes richer theming, grouped multi-model comparisons, helpers common modelling packages.

what: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
text: Compose side table onto forest plot plot styled.

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
code: add_forest_table(   plot = NULL,   position = c("left", "right"),   show_terms = TRUE,   show_n = NULL,   show_estimate = TRUE,   term_header = "Term",   n_header = "N",   estimate_label = "Estimate",   digits = NULL,   text_size = NULL,   striped_rows = NULL,   stripe_fill = NULL,   stripe_colour = NULL )

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
text: plot plot created ggforestplot(). Leave NULL use + add_forest_table(...) syntax. position Whether place table left right forest plot. show_terms Whether show term column table. show_n Whether show N column. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data include n column. show_estimate Whether show formatted estimate confidence interval column. term_header Header text term column. n_header Header text N column. estimate_label Header label estimate column. digits Number digits used formatting estimates. Defaults 2. text_size Text size table contents. Defaults 3.2. striped_rows Whether draw alternating row stripes behind table. Defaults stripe setting used ggforestplot(). stripe_fill Fill colour used striped rows. Defaults stripe fill used ggforestplot(). stripe_colour Outline colour striped rows. Defaults stripe outline used ggforestplot().

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
text: patchwork-composed plot containing forest plot side table, ggplot add-object plot = NULL.

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.3, -0.2, 0.4),   conf.low = c(0.1, -0.4, 0.2),   conf.high = c(0.5, 0.0, 0.6),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98) )  p <- ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size") add_forest_table(p, position = "left", show_n = TRUE, estimate_label = "Beta")   ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size") +   add_forest_table(position = "right", show_n = TRUE, estimate_label = "Beta")

what: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: Standardizes coefficient table internal forest-plot data structure.

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
code: as_forest_data(   data,   term,   estimate,   conf.low,   conf.high,   label = term,   group = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   separator_group = NULL,   n = NULL,   p.value = NULL,   exponentiate = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending") )

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: data data frame containing coefficient estimates intervals. term Column name holding model term identifier. estimate Column name holding point estimate. conf.low Column name holding lower confidence bound. conf.high Column name holding upper confidence bound. label Optional column name used displayed row label. group Optional column name used color-grouping multiple estimates per row. grouping Optional column name used split rows grouped plot sections. separator_group Optional column name used identify contiguous multi-row blocks can outlined separator lines. n Optional column name holding sample sizes N labels attached tables. p.value Optional column name holding p-values. exponentiate Logical; TRUE, require positive values estimates intervals. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending".

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: standardized data frame columns ready ggforestplot(). returned structure includes separator_group metadata supplied.

what: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: Builds forest plot standardized coefficient data directly fitted model, optional grouped sections, striped rows, separator lines labeled variable blocks, point-shape staple controls.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
code: ggforestplot(   data,   term = "term",   estimate = "estimate",   conf.low = "conf.low",   conf.high = "conf.high",   label = term,   group = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   separator_group = NULL,   n = NULL,   p.value = NULL,   exponentiate = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending"),   point_size = 2.3,   point_shape = 19,   line_size = 0.5,   staple_width = 0.2,   dodge_width = 0.6,   separator_lines = FALSE,   separator_line_linetype = 2,   separator_line_colour = "black",   separator_line_size = 0.4,   striped_rows = FALSE,   stripe_fill = "grey95",   stripe_colour = NA,   zero_line = TRUE,   zero_line_linetype = 2,   zero_line_colour = "grey60",   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   title = NULL )

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: data Either tidy coefficient data frame model object supported broom::tidy(). term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, label, group, grouping, separator_group, n, p.value Column names used data data frame. exponentiate Logical; TRUE, draw estimate axis log scale null line 1. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending". point_size Point size coefficient markers. point_shape Shape used coefficient markers. line_size Line width confidence intervals. staple_width Width terminal staples confidence interval lines. dodge_width Horizontal dodging used grouped estimates. separator_lines Logical; TRUE, draw dashed separator lines around labeled block identified separator_group. separator_line_linetype Line type used separator lines. separator_line_colour Colour used separator lines. separator_line_size Line width used separator lines. striped_rows Logical; TRUE, shade alternating rows. stripe_fill Fill color used shaded rows. stripe_colour Border color shaded rows. zero_line Logical; TRUE, draw null reference line. zero_line_linetype Line type null reference line. zero_line_colour Color null reference line. xlab, ylab, title Optional plot labels.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: ggplot object. Use add_forest_table() compose side table styling plot.

what: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: Uses broom::tidy() convert fitted model forest-plot data.

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
code: tidy_forest_model(   model,   conf.int = TRUE,   conf.level = 0.95,   exponentiate = FALSE,   intercept = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending") )

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: model fitted model object supported broom::tidy(). conf.int Logical; TRUE, request confidence intervals broom::tidy(). conf.level Confidence level intervals. exponentiate Logical; passed broom::tidy(). intercept Logical; FALSE, drop intercept term. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending".

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: standardized coefficient data frame ready ggforestplot().

title: ggforestplotR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added grouped section support forest plots. Added alternating row striping. Added add_forest_table() side-table composition. Added point shape confidence-interval staple width controls. Added package documentation, tests, getting-started vignette.
